intro: A new wave of art-pop, electronic, and experimental musicians is dissolving the wall between opera and the avant-garde — drawing on conservatory training, baroque arias, choral traditions, and orchestral collaboration to push popular music into operatic territory. From FKA twigs's childhood opera lessons to Caroline Polachek's baroque coaching to Holly Herndon's Sacred Harp choirs and Lyra Pramuk's all-vocal symphonies, these artists treat the trained voice as the next frontier for electronic and pop music. The result is a generation that hears no contradiction between Handel and hyperpop.

artists.name: Caroline Polachek
artists.classical_or_opera_connection: Polachek has trained for over a decade in classical and baroque vocal technique with an opera coach, weaving Handelian melisma and operatic belting into avant-pop tracks like 'Welcome to My Island' and 'Ocean of Tears.'
artists.evidence_quote: I love how the voice just becomes the ultimate analogue synth in opera.
artists.representative_track_or_album: Desire, I Want to Turn Into You (2023)

artists.name: FKA twigs
artists.classical_or_opera_connection: Trained in classical ballet from age 7 and in operatic singing in Latin as a teenager, twigs threads operatic phrasing and 'fallen heroine' drama through her Eusexua-era work.
artists.evidence_quote: Electronic music, dance, opera, martial arts: FKA twigs says 'this is just my makeup.'

artists.name: Holly Herndon
artists.classical_or_opera_connection: Raised on church choir in East Tennessee and trained at Mills and Stanford, Herndon stages 'training ceremonies' where a live choir teaches her AI 'Spawn' to sing — fusing Sacred Harp shape-note tradition with machine learning on PROTO.
artists.evidence_quote: I went to an international Sacred Harp Meetup in Berlin where I live... When I stood in the middle the power of the voices brought me to tears. It was partly pure sonics and the beauty of the songs, but also the sight of witnessing people from all different backgrounds come together under such a simple and elegant premise.

artists.name: Lyra Pramuk
artists.classical_or_opera_connection: A classically trained vocalist (Eastman School of Music) who built her debut Fountain entirely from layered, processed recordings of her own trained voice — proposing a post-human choral music born from gospel, Gregorian chant, and contemporary classical practice.
artists.evidence_quote: I sang religious music and Black gospel in a number of choirs until I was 23. I would say that was my main musical experience.

artists.name: Oklou
artists.classical_or_opera_connection: Conservatory-trained on piano and cello and a choir singer from age four, Oklou braids French baroque polyphony and choral textures into hyper-modern pop on choke enough.
artists.evidence_quote: It's hard for me to identify how much my classical background has trained my brain to be sensitive to certain chord progressions, harmonies, and melodies.

artists.name: ANOHNI
artists.classical_or_opera_connection: A career built around quasi-classical, baroque-pop instrumentation; ANOHNI has performed with the Opera Orchestra of Lyon, the Roma Sinfonietta, the Manchester Camerata, and presented major works at the Royal Opera House (London), Teatro Real (Madrid), and the Sydney Opera House.
artists.evidence_quote: The ultimate draw is [Anohni's] voice... a multi-octave siren that would sound painfully lovely no matter what [she] was saying.
artists.evidence_source_name: Pitchfork (via I Am a Bird Now Wikipedia citation)

artists.name: Arca
artists.classical_or_opera_connection: Cited Wagner's Der Ring des Nibelungen as a primary inspiration for the four-album KICK cycle, and writes operatic ballads in Spanish (e.g. 'No Queda Nada,' 'Calor') alongside her club productions.
artists.evidence_quote: [Arca] cited German composer Richard Wagner's Der Ring des Nibelungen as a primary inspiration for the Kick series.

artists.name: Sevdaliza
artists.classical_or_opera_connection: The Iranian-Dutch singer marries trip-hop and electronic production with classical string arrangements and a vibrato-rich vocal that critics liken to opera and Persian classical music — even though she has had no formal vocal training.
artists.evidence_quote: I've never had a singing lesson in my life, and I've never listened to Persian music in my life! It's really interesting to me that some things just come to you unconsciously like that.

artists.name: Eartheater
artists.classical_or_opera_connection: Suzuki-method violin training, exposure to Byzantine microtonality from her Greek Orthodox upbringing, and a three-octave range have made Alexandra Drewchin one of the most overtly operatic voices in experimental pop, especially across Phoenix and Powders.
artists.evidence_quote: On 'Powders', Eartheater explores every dimension of her voice — breathy, belting, pleading, yelping, staccato chorales, operatic trilling.
artists.representative_track_or_album: Phoenix: Flames Are Dew Upon My Skin (2020)

artists.name: Klein
artists.classical_or_opera_connection: The London experimentalist has scored and staged her own original musicals (Care, premiered at the ICA in 2018) and has released work on the prestigious classical label Pentatone, layering operatic textures and chorales into manipulated R&B and noise.
artists.evidence_quote: Klein has made stage and film versions of a musical, Care, and released music on the prestigious classical label Pentatone.

artists.name: L'Rain
artists.classical_or_opera_connection: Taja Cheek studied classical cello and piano before pursuing experimental music; critics describe her records as 'baroque dreamscapes' that draw on choral and string-quartet textures.
artists.evidence_quote: If someone can't pin down what you are, then you can be whatever you want to be.

artists.name: Patrick Belaga
artists.classical_or_opera_connection: A trained cellist-composer who scores film and dance, Belaga lives squarely in the crossover lane — working from Iannis Xenakis and Benjamin Britten on the classical side and improvising with electronics and field recordings on records like Blutt.
artists.evidence_quote: In 2019, Belaga released his first album, Groundswell, influenced by Greek composer Iannis Xenakis and 20th-century classical musician Benjamin Britten.
